home.title: Virtual Medical Laboratory
home.tagline: Choose a lab. Complete each experiment step by step — practise or take the exam.
home.footer: {done} of 6 labs ready · the rest are coming soon

1.title: Bacterial cell morphology
1.subtitle: Simple stain — methylene blue
1.desc: Prepare a smear, fix it and stain with methylene blue to study the cell shape under the microscope.

2.title: Gram staining method
2.subtitle: Differential staining
2.desc: Use crystal violet, Lugol, alcohol and fuchsin to separate Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria.

3.title: Drigalski plating
3.subtitle: Spreader inoculation — pure culture
3.desc: Spread the suspension across 3 plates in turn to isolate single colonies and obtain a pure culture, then Gram stain.

4.title: Antibiotic sensitivity
4.subtitle: Paper-disk (disk-diffusion) method
4.desc: Lawn-inoculate the culture, place antibiotic disks, measure the zones of inhibition and determine sensitivity.

5.title: Studying living microorganisms
5.subtitle: «Crushed-drop» preparation
5.desc: Prepare a crushed-drop mount of living bacteria in saline, cover it with a cover slip and observe motility at ×40.

6.title: Ziehl-Neelsen method
6.subtitle: Acid-fast staining
6.desc: Detect the tuberculosis bacillus (AFB) with carbol fuchsin, acid and methylene blue.

ui.learnDesc: Guidance and the next tool are shown at every step. No score — practise freely.
ui.examDescPdf: First arrange the procedure, then perform it unaided. Scored by the PDF criteria (100 total).
ui.examDescStages: First arrange the procedure, then perform it unaided. Scored by stages (100 total).
ui.planInstruction: Place the steps in the correct order.
ui.planLocked: Once confirmed, the order can't be changed.
ui.planFirst: First arrange the procedure in the right panel →
ui.dragHintSidebar: Drag the tools onto the bench and place them anywhere
ui.dragToTable: Drag the tools from the left panel onto the bench
ui.examBanner: Exam — no help. Do it as best you can.

hint.degrease: Pass the slide through the spirit-lamp flame to degrease it
hint.saline: Place a drop of physiological saline (NaCl) on the centre of the slide
hint.flame: Sterilise the inoculation loop in the flame
hint.charge: With the cooled loop, lightly touch and take culture
hint.mix: Mix the culture into the drop until even
hint.cover: Cover the drop with the cover slip (lower it on edge)
hint.blot: Blot the excess fluid with filter paper
hint.observe: Take the prep to the microscope and view it with the ×40 objective

toast.needBridge: First place the glass bridge on the bench
toast.needPrep: First prepare the slide and cover it with the cover slip
toast.success: ✓ The prep is ready — you completed the task successfully!

steps.degreaseResult: Clean the slide and degrease it by passing it through the spirit-lamp flame
steps.salineResult: Place a drop of sterile physiological saline on the centre of the slide
steps.loopResult: Flame the loop, cool it and lightly take culture
steps.mixResult: Mix the culture into the drop until an even (homogeneous) suspension
steps.coverResult: Cover the drop with the cover slip (lowered on edge, no bubbles) and blot the excess with filter paper
steps.microResult: Examine the prep with the ×40 objective and determine motility

score.loopPartial: The loop was used without sterilising
score.mixZero: The culture was not mixed into the drop
score.coverPartial: The excess fluid was not blotted with filter paper
score.microZero: The prep was not viewed under the microscope

micro.question: Is this bacterium motile?
micro.revealMotile: The bacterium swims actively and directionally — this is true motility (via flagella), different from Brownian motion.
micro.revealNonmotile: The bacterium stays in place with only Brownian jitter — it is a non-motile species.
